title: High Molecular Weight DNA Extraction of Pomegranate
author: Mariano Resendiz
materials: Qubit 4 Fluorometer to measure DNA concentration:

steps.title: Place 4 autoclaved mortars/pestles at room...
steps.instruction: Place 4 autoclaved mortars/pestles at room temp and then at -80°C (for at least 30 min; overnight preferred).

steps.title: Split 10 g into approximate 4 × 2
steps.instruction: Split 10 g into approximate 4 × 2.5 g batches or just grind half a 50 mL tube in a larger pestle with about 1 gram of sand for each prep.

steps.title: For each mortar
steps.instruction: For each mortar: Add ~2.5 g of flash frozen tissue in liquid nitrogen (LN2)

steps.title: Once the LN2 begins to dissipate...
steps.instruction: Once the LN2 begins to dissipate add a small amount of NIB ~10 mL straight into the mortar and crush the NIB and tissue amalgamation into a fine powder once again. These cold conditions arrest redox reactions in this process.

steps.title: Pour light green powder into 50...
steps.instruction: Pour light green powder into 50 mL tubes with an autoclaved spatula or sterile tool.

steps.title: Add enough NIB (with BME) to...
steps.instruction: Add enough NIB (with BME) to even out each tube to the same mL level, swirl to homogenize (can be 15 min total); this helps to melt any remaining ice crystals. (You may have between 20-25 mL at this step)

steps.title: Add about 10 mL of SWB...
steps.instruction: Add about 10 mL of SWB to each tube and invert the tubes to mix pellet and SWB.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at a rate...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at a rate of 4000 g, at 4°C, and for 10 minutes. Remove supernatant

steps.title: Repeat the SWB step (17) to...
steps.instruction: Repeat the SWB step (17) to further remove tissue and remove supernatant.

steps.title: If tissue (and DNA) is limiting,...
steps.instruction: If tissue (and DNA) is limiting, discard the supernatants into a collection tube.

steps.title: Add 20 mL Carlson lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL Carlson lysis buffer (with BME) to each pellet; invert to homogenize.

steps.title: Incubate 55-65°C, 1 hr, invert GENTLY...
steps.instruction: Incubate 55-65°C, 1 hr, invert GENTLY after 30 min and add 12 µL of RNase A per tube if you have between 20-25 mL of CLB in the tube - if you have more adjust to the ratio. The DNA is at its most fragile state at this step.

steps.title: Cool to room temp (~10 min)
steps.instruction: Cool to room temp (~10 min). This is required! Do not skip this step.

steps.title: Add 20 mL of Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl (PCI)...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL of Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl (PCI) , shake gently to incorporate - 20x inversions ok, centrifuge 4000g, 4°C, 15 min.

steps.title: Pool upper aqueous layers of the...
steps.instruction: Pool upper aqueous layers of the same variety into new tubes while avoiding the interphase layer of the PCI.

steps.title: Add ~14-20 mL of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Add ~14-20 mL of freshly prepared and ice cold isopropanol (0.7x volume) to ~15-20mL of product, invert 10x.

steps.title: Centrifuge at a rate of 10000g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at a rate of 10000g, at 4°C, and for 45 minutes.

steps.title: Discard supernatant, add 20 mL of...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant, add 20 mL of freshly prepared and ice-cold 80% ethanol, invert 10x, centrifuge at a rate of 5000 g, at 4°C, and for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Repeat ethanol wash
steps.instruction: Repeat ethanol wash.

steps.title: Air dry pellet(s) on an autoclaved kimwipe for 5-10 minutes
steps.instruction: Air dry pellet(s) on an autoclaved kimwipe for 5-10 minutes.

steps.title: Resuspend in 200 µL of TE...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 200 µL of TE or EB (overnight preferred) and then perform quality control.

steps.title: Nanodrop to measure 260/280 and 260/230...
steps.instruction: Nanodrop to measure 260/280 and 260/230 ratio 260/280 ratio around 1.8-2.0 260/230 ratio between 2.0-2.2

steps.title: Yield
steps.instruction: Yield : ~20 µg Volume : 200 µL Concentration : 100 ng/µL (>100 ng/µL target)

steps.title: Check
steps.instruction: Check : Measure concentration (Qubit/Nanodrop) post-resuspension. Measure QC ratios with Nanodrop first Once Nanodrop QC is adequate measure concentration with Qubit or send through tapestation

steps.title: Add 199 μL of Qubit Kit Buffer to two 0
steps.instruction: Add 199 μL of Qubit Kit Buffer to two 0.5 mL tubes and an additional tube for each DNA sample, which will serve as the working solution tubes.

steps.title: Add 1 μL ds DNA HS...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL ds DNA HS to all tubes, then vortex mix tubes for 2-3 seconds.

steps.title: Transfer 190 μL from each of...
steps.instruction: Transfer 190 μL from each of the two working solution tubes to two new 0.5 mL tubes, which will serve as the standard solutions.

steps.title: Add 10 μL of Standard 1...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of Standard 1 into one of the standard solution tubes, now labeled S1, and 10 μL of Standard 2 into the other standard solution tube, now labeled S2, then vortex mix 2-3 seconds.

steps.title: Add 199 μL from each of...
steps.instruction: Add 199 μL from each of the other working solutions, designated for the DNA samples, to new tubes.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of the corresponding...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of the corresponding DNA sample to its designated tube, then vortex mix 2-3 seconds.

steps.title: Incubate all standard and DNA sample...
steps.instruction: Incubate all standard and DNA sample tubes for 2 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Calibrate Qubit 4 Fluorometer by selecting...
steps.instruction: Calibrate Qubit 4 Fluorometer by selecting ds DNA HS and inserting Standard 1, then Standard 2. (There should be a graph with an increasing line)

steps.title: After calibration, insert a DNA sample...
steps.instruction: After calibration, insert a DNA sample and press "Read tube", then record the concentration in ng/μL.

steps.title: Repeat for each DNA sample
steps.instruction: Repeat for each DNA sample.

steps.title: Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB) - 200 mL Limits chemical interactions
steps.instruction: Nuclei Isolation Buffer (NIB) - 200 mL Limits chemical interactions

steps.title: Sorbitol Wash Buffer (SWB) - 250...
steps.instruction: Sorbitol Wash Buffer (SWB) - 250 mL Efficiently washes leaf tissue of extreme impurities.

steps.title: Carlson Lysis Buffer - 200 mL...
steps.instruction: Carlson Lysis Buffer - 200 mL Breaks open cellular membranes to release DNA.

steps.title: TE/EB Buffer - 100 mL Final solution to store DNA
steps.instruction: TE/EB Buffer - 100 mL Final solution to store DNA

steps.title: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid...
steps.instruction: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid reagents at a liquid phase for 20 minutes (L20)

steps.title: The base is created by adding...
steps.instruction: The base is created by adding 2 mL of 1 M Tris pH 9.5, 4 mL of 0.5 M EDTA, and 20 mL of 1 M KCl. Then, add water to the 200 mL mark. If particulate matter is present (dust, microplastics) filter the solution. Add a magnetic stir bar.

steps.title: Autoclave, and then after autoclaving, add...
steps.instruction: Autoclave, and then after autoclaving, add 2 grams of PVP-360, 0.116 g. of spermidine, 0.040 g. of spermine, and 34.23 g. of sucrose.

steps.title: On the day of use, add 2 mL of BME
steps.instruction: On the day of use, add 2 mL of BME.

steps.title: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid...
steps.instruction: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid reagents in a liquid phase for 20 minutes (L20)

steps.title: The base is created by adding 2
steps.instruction: The base is created by adding 2.5 mL of 1 M Tris pH 9.5, 5 mL of 0.5 M EDTA, and 25 mL of 1 M KCl. Then, add water to the 250 mL mark. (Note this is similar to NIB) If particulate matter is present filter the solution. Add a magnetic stir bar.

steps.title: Autoclave, and then after autoclaving, add 16 g
steps.instruction: Autoclave, and then after autoclaving, add 16 g. of sorbitol, 2.5 g. of PVP-360, and 42.79 g. of sucrose.

steps.title: On day of use, add 2
steps.instruction: On day of use, add 2.5 mL of BME.

steps.title: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid...
steps.instruction: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid reagents in a liquid phase for 20 minutes (L20)

steps.title: Create the base by adding 20...
steps.instruction: Create the base by adding 20 mL of 1 M Tris pH 9.5, 4 mL of 0.5 M EDTA, 56 mL of 5 M NaCl, 4 g. of CTAB, and water to the 200 mL mark. Add a magnetic stirrer. Autoclave at L20, filter if needed, and then add 2 g. of PEG 8000.

steps.title: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid...
steps.instruction: Begin by autoclaving all heat-resistant liquid reagents in a liquid phase for 20 minutes (L20)

steps.title: [TE] Create the base by adding...
steps.instruction: [TE] Create the base by adding 1 mL of 1 M Tris pH 9.5, 0.2 mL of 0.5 M EDTA, water to the 100 mL mark, and pH to 8.0 with HCl.

steps.title: On the day of use, combine...
steps.instruction: On the day of use, combine with 500 µL of BME and add 12 µL of RNase A diluted to 100 μg/mL in resuspension buffer (50 mM Tris·Cl, pH 8.0; 10 mM EDTA)

steps.title: Autoclave mortars, pestles, kimwipes in a...
steps.instruction: Autoclave mortars, pestles, kimwipes in a foil packet, sand, glass rods or metal spatulas, autoclave 5 mL and 1 mL tips if needed all at a gaseous phase G30 on the autoclave.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 3M NaOAc...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 3M NaOAc and incubate in -20°C for 1 hour.

steps.title: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer
steps.instruction: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer

steps.title: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer
steps.instruction: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer

steps.title: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer
steps.instruction: Let the solution mix overnight on an automatic stirrer.

steps.title: Add 1 g of sand to each mortar
steps.instruction: Add 1 g of sand to each mortar

steps.title: Grind tissue, sand, and LN2 until...
steps.instruction: Grind tissue, sand, and LN2 until a light green powder develops

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at a rate...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at a rate of 4000 g, at 4°C, and for 10 minutes. Remove supernatant

steps.title: Repeat the PCI cleanup if any...
steps.instruction: Repeat the PCI cleanup if any of the product thus far prepared is brown (oxidized)

steps.title: Extraction can be completed within a...
steps.instruction: Extraction can be completed within a day, typically in six hours. Budget for this as you start this section.
